name: Rahul Chawla
questions_asked: Alien Dictionary, Clone a graph, write an SQL query to find the second highest salary.
tips: Focus heavily on Dynamic Programming and Graphs. Do at least 200 medium level LeetCode questions.

name: Priya Kapoor
questions_asked: Reverse a linked list in groups of K, Word Ladder problem, SQL queries on Joins.
tips: Communication is key. If you are stuck, think out loud. Interviewers care more about your approach than the final code.

name: Amit Rao
questions_asked: Find the longest palindromic substring, Implement a Trie, explain multithreading concepts in Java.
tips: Make sure your resume doesn't have fake projects. They grilled me on every single technology I mentioned.

name: Sneha Yadav
questions_asked: Design an elevator system (LLD), Valid Parentheses, Page replacement algorithms in OS.
tips: System Design basics are crucial. Read 'Designing Data-Intensive Applications' or watch standard YouTube crash courses.

name: Vikram Rao
questions_asked: Design an elevator system (LLD), Valid Parentheses, Page replacement algorithms in OS.
tips: Practice mock interviews with your friends. Answering behavioral questions smoothly using the STAR method makes a huge difference.

name: Neha Bose
questions_asked: Alien Dictionary, Clone a graph, write an SQL query to find the second highest salary.
tips: Speed matters in the online assessment. Don't spend too much time optimizing early on; get a working brute force solution first.

name: Rohit Rao
questions_asked: Two Sum, Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock, how does a hash map work internally.
tips: Brush up on core CS subjects: OS, DBMS, and Computer Networks. They asked me very specific SQL indexing questions.

name: Anjali Mehta
questions_asked: Longest Increasing Subsequence, Edit Distance, explain the event loop in JavaScript.
tips: Stay calm during the Bar Raiser round. They will push you to your limits to see how you handle pressure and ambiguity.

name: Karan Singh
questions_asked: Longest Increasing Subsequence, Edit Distance, explain the event loop in JavaScript.
tips: Don't ignore Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) concepts. I was asked to design a parking lot system from scratch.

name: Pooja Nair
questions_asked: Detect cycle in a directed graph, LRU Cache implementation, questions on React hooks.
tips: Maintain a good CGPA (above 8 ideally). It acts as a tie-breaker when many students solve the OA questions.

name: Arjun Nair
questions_asked: Design a URL shortener, Find the median in a stream of integers, differences between TCP and UDP.
tips: Contribute to open source! Mentioning my merged PRs in popular repos completely drove the technical interview.

name: Kavya Patel
questions_asked: Two Sum, Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock, how does a hash map work internally.
tips: For front-end roles, know JavaScript under the hood. Closures, event loop, and promises are guaranteed questions.

name: Varun Gupta
questions_asked: Trapping Rain Water, Next Greater Element, normalization forms in databases.
tips: Have a solid grasp of REST API principles. I was asked to architect an API for a chat application on the whiteboard.

name: Riya Kumar
questions_asked: Trapping Rain Water, Next Greater Element, normalization forms in databases.
tips: Always clarify the question before jumping into the code. Asking edge-case questions shows maturity.

name: Aditya Gupta
questions_asked: Merge K sorted lists, Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum, Explain ACID properties in DBMS.
tips: Know your database transactions. I had a 20-minute discussion just on ACID properties and isolation levels.

name: Shruti Sharma
questions_asked: Design an elevator system (LLD), Valid Parentheses, Page replacement algorithms in OS.
tips: If you mention AWS or Cloud on your resume, be ready to explain how load balancers and auto-scaling work.

name: Siddharth Bose
questions_asked: Reverse a linked list in groups of K, Word Ladder problem, SQL queries on Joins.
tips: Don't just memorize LeetCode solutions. Understand the underlying patterns like sliding window and two pointers.

name: Aisha Patel
questions_asked: Detect cycle in a directed graph, LRU Cache implementation, questions on React hooks.
tips: Read up on the company's core values before the HR round. Align your personal stories with their mission statement.

name: Dev Iyer
questions_asked: Two Sum, Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock, how does a hash map work internally.
tips: During the machine coding round, focus on modularity and clean code. Hardcoded values will lead to rejection.

name: Tanya Das
questions_asked: Detect cycle in a directed graph, LRU Cache implementation, questions on React hooks.
tips: Be honest if you don't know an answer. Saying 'I don't know, but here is how I would approach it' is highly respected.

name: Akash Kapoor
questions_asked: Design an elevator system (LLD), Valid Parentheses, Page replacement algorithms in OS.
tips: Have 2-3 strong questions prepared for the interviewer at the end. It shows you are genuinely interested in the role.

name: Ishita Yadav
questions_asked: Longest Increasing Subsequence, Edit Distance, explain the event loop in JavaScript.
tips: Master Git commands. Being able to explain branching strategies and merge conflict resolution impressed my interviewer.

name: Yash Mehta
questions_asked: Longest Increasing Subsequence, Edit Distance, explain the event loop in JavaScript.
tips: For product companies, think about edge cases in the user experience, not just the technical edge cases.

name: Tanvi Iyer
questions_asked: Trapping Rain Water, Next Greater Element, normalization forms in databases.
tips: If you're interviewing for a backend role, make sure you know the difference between monolithic and microservice architectures.

name: Pranav Sharma
questions_asked: Detect cycle in a directed graph, LRU Cache implementation, questions on React hooks.
tips: Participate in competitive programming. The speed you gain there will make online assessments feel very easy.

name: Rashi Saxena
questions_asked: Longest Increasing Subsequence, Edit Distance, explain the event loop in JavaScript.
tips: Keep your GitHub profile active and clean. Interviewers sometimes look at your code quality before the interview.

name: Karthik Joshi
questions_asked: Two Sum, Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock, how does a hash map work internally.
tips: Know how to test your own code. Writing unit tests during the interview sets you apart from 90% of candidates.

name: Roshni Mehta
questions_asked: Reverse a linked list in groups of K, Word Ladder problem, SQL queries on Joins.
tips: Understand web security basics. Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and SQL injection were major discussion points in my tech round.

name: Aman Malhotra
questions_asked: Two Sum, Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock, how does a hash map work internally.
tips: Don't neglect your soft skills. Being likable and easy to work with is just as important as your technical abilities.

name: Megha Bose
questions_asked: Design a URL shortener, Find the median in a stream of integers, differences between TCP and UDP.
tips: Finally, don't burn out. Take breaks during preparation. A fresh mind performs much better in interviews than an exhausted one.
